inline_comments.message: Currently there are no jobs that actually produce any kind of SNAPSHOT versions for the images under test here so I will have to add those before any of this can work
I actually have serious doubts about how this works with review branches that are checked out in Jenkins for verification - I will need to investigate this further

Solved this by combination of GERRIT_BRANCH (which should give the branch in Jenkins), git rev-parse command (which should give the branch locally if not in any detached head or funny local branch) and defaulting to the master branch later after cloning if there is no integration/csit branch corresponding to the local branch (as master should always be used in such case). Not pretty, but should cover all situations.
This seems a pretty convoluted way of finding the version.properties regardless of who runs the script and from where, but I couldn't immediately figure out anything better
